title: alyssum montanum subsp. epiroticum
intro: Alyssum montanum subsp. epiroticum, also known as Epirotic Madwort, is a subspecies of the annual flowering plant Alyssum montanum that belongs to the mustard family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Alyssum montanum subsp. epiroticum include treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as soothing skin irritations and wounds. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
